debug_points.description: The camera appears to be positioned over water, likely the Panama Canal or Bay of Panama based on the coordinates. All images show dark water surface with no road infrastructure visible. The downward view (image 9) shows murky water with some light reflection, no pavement whatsoever.

The Street View imagery appears to be captured from water level, showing a body of water (pond or lake) with well-maintained grass edges and a concrete or paved edge visible in image 1. Images 4-6 show a road with white lane markings adjacent to the water. No potholes or road damage visible - this appears to be a landscaped water feature near a road.

These images appear to be taken from an unusual angle, possibly looking down at a concrete surface or structure. The visible surfaces show grey concrete with some joints/seams and what appears to be yellow paint or markings in some frames. No potholes or road damage with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 images.

No street view imagery is available for this location. All 9 images display the Google Maps 'Sorry, we have no imagery here' message, indicating this area has not been covered by Street View vehicles or the coordinates fall in an unmapped zone.

These images appear to be severely distorted or corrupted Google Street View captures, showing motion blur and stretched/smeared imagery that makes the road surface completely unreadable. The images show diagonal streaks and blurred bands of color rather than recognizable road features. No road infrastructure, potholes, or surface damage can be identified.

The images show a flooded urban street intersection with extensive standing water covering the roadway. While the water obscures much of the road surface, the visible portions show yellow painted curbs, brick paver sidewalks, and cracked concrete/asphalt surfaces. No actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth can be confirmed - only standing water on what appears to be intact but aged pavement.

These images show extremely close-up views of a vehicle (appears to be a tour bus or promotional vehicle) covered in sponsor logos and branding decals including Tucán Country Club & Resort, Thrifty Car Rental, Hertz, Ricardo Perez S.A., and Panama tourism logos. No road surface is visible in any meaningful way to assess for potholes or road damage.

These images show a flooded area with standing water covering what appears to be a road or paved surface. The water is murky gray-brown and obscures the road surface entirely. While concrete/asphalt edges are visible in some frames (particularly images 1, 5, and 8), no actual potholes with visible depth, broken edges, or exposed base material can be confirmed beneath the water.

Large puddle of standing water covering most of the intersection area. The water is opaque/murky beige-brown color and no hole edges, depth, exposed base material, or broken asphalt is visible beneath or through the water surface. Yellow painted curb and metal railing visible at street corner.

The camera appears to be positioned over or in a body of water, likely a pond, lake, or flooded area. All images show murky greenish-grey water with shoreline/edge visible in some directions showing concrete curbing, grass, plants, and paved walkway areas. No road surface is visible to assess for potholes.

The imagery shows extremely close-up views of road surface, appearing to be taken from water level or very low angle. The road surface visible consists of grey asphalt and concrete sidewalk/curb areas. No potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 images.

All images show extreme motion blur from a moving vehicle, making the road surface completely unreadable. The visible portions show asphalt pavement with white road markings (Image 7) and what appears to be a concrete or paved surface edge in some frames, but no discernible potholes or road damage can be identified due to the severe image distortion.

These images appear to be taken from an elevated position looking down at a paved surface, possibly an airport runway or large parking area. The surface shows consistent grey asphalt with painted yellow curb markings visible in images 7 and 8. No potholes, holes, or missing asphalt are visible in any of the 9 images.

The camera appears to be positioned very low or submerged, showing what looks like murky water or a flooded surface. The surrounding concrete edges visible in some directional views show weathered pavement but no visible potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges.

The images show a parking lot area with a silver/grey compact car. The pavement surface appears to be concrete or asphalt with some visible cracks (Image 3-4), but no actual potholes with missing asphalt or exposed base material are visible. The dark greenish-grey areas in the lower portions of the images appear to be rendering artifacts or blurred/obscured sections, not actual road damage.

The camera appears to be positioned very low or partially submerged, looking at what seems to be a large puddle of standing water covering most of the road surface. While water is pooling extensively, there is no visible evidence of actual potholes - no exposed base material, broken edges, or holes with depth are visible through or around the water.

These images appear to be taken from an extremely low angle or obstructed camera position, likely mounted on or very close to a vehicle (red car visible in images 2-4). Most views show only the car body, road surface at an extreme angle, or blurred/obscured areas. The visible road sections in images 5-8 show asphalt with yellow road markings (crosswalk stripes) but no potholes are visible.
